Albert Boxler Brand Riesling

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Albert BoxlerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Boxler's Brand Grand Cru Riesling, from steep granite slopes above Turckheim. Warmer-site fruit than Sommerberg but with the same precise dry register.

Cuvée page →

Albert Boxler Sommerberg Riesling E

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Albert BoxlerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Boxler's E parcel selection on Sommerberg, a 70-year-old vine block on steep south-facing granite above Niedermorschwihr. Sharp, mineral, slow to age.

Cuvée page →

Albert Boxler Sommerberg Riesling L

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Domaine Albert BoxlerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Boxler's L parcel selection on Sommerberg (formerly called Jeunes Vignes), younger granite vines but the same incisive mineral character.

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Albert MannAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

Albert Mann's selection from the heart of Schlossberg, the original 1.5 hectare epicentre of the Grand Cru. Biodynamic, structured for the cellar.

Barmes Buecher Cremant d Alsace Extra Brut

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Festive · Elegant

Domaine Barmes-BuecherAlsace€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Barmes-Buecher's vintage-dated extra-brut Cremant. Long-aged on lees in a Champagne-style Alsace Cremant, biodynamic, often zero-dosage.

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Barmes-BuecherAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Barmes-Buecher's Hengst Riesling from calcareous-marl slopes above Wintzenheim. Biodynamic, limestone-driven, dry and slowly developing.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Beck-HartwegAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Beck-Hartweg's Frankstein Riesling from coarse granite around Dambach-la-Ville. Biodynamic, dry, with the brisk, light register of Dambach granite.

Bernard Bohn Riesling Schiste

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Funky · Elegant

Domaine BohnAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Bernard Bohn's natural-style Schist Riesling from Reichsfeld in the Lower Vosges. Biodynamic, smoky, mineral; one of the few Steige-schist bottlings in Alsace.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Bott-GeylAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Bott-Geyl's Mandelberg Riesling from south-facing limestone-marl slopes near Mittelwihr, named for the almond trees that once dotted the slope.

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Bott-GeylAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Bott-Geyl's Schoenenbourg Riesling from marl-gypsum slopes above Riquewihr. Concentrated and saline with the cool-climate cut of the cru.

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Bruno SorgAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Bruno Sorg's Florimont Riesling from limestone slopes near Ingersheim. The cru takes its name from 'flower hill' for its rich Vosges flora.

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Funky · Elegant

Domaine Christian BinnerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Binner's natural-style Schlossberg Riesling. Bone-dry, granite-driven, often with a slight reductive twang from the no-added-sulfite winemaking.

Clos Sainte-Hune

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Maison TrimbachAlsace€€€€€€€€€€Still White

Trimbach's 1.67 hectare monopole inside Grand Cru Rosacker, declassified to Alsace AOC by family preference. Piercing dry Riesling that needs a decade to unwind into wax, citrus and Vosges granite salinity.

Dirler-Cade's Kitterle Riesling from terraced sandstone slopes above Guebwiller. Smoky, dry, structured; biodynamic since the mid 2000s.

Dirler-Cade's Saering Riesling from sandstone slopes around Guebwiller. Biodynamic and bone-dry; the maison's everyday Grand Cru.

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant

Famille HugelAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Grossi Laue (Alsatian for 'great growth') is Hugel's bottling from their oldest plots in Schoenenbourg and Pflostig. Dry, stony, structured, built to age.

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Famille HugelAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

A single 0.45 hectare parcel high on Grand Cru Schoenenbourg, declassified to Alsace AOC because Hugel disagrees with the wider GC delimitation. Hugel's flagship dry Riesling, released as a multi-decade vintage statement.

Marc Kreydenweiss's Wiebelsberg Riesling from sandstone slopes above Andlau. The sandstone counterpart to Kastelberg's schist; lighter, more aerial.

Deiss's complanted Langenberg from sunny calcareous slopes, Premier Cru tier. Riesling-led and more austere than Engelgarten.

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Marcel DeissAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

Deiss's complanted Schoenenbourg above Riquewihr, marl-and-gypsum soils, Riesling-dominant. Cooler and more racy than Altenberg or Mambourg.

★★★★★★★★★★

Andre Ostertag's Heissenberg Riesling from sandstone slopes near Nothalten, alongside Muenchberg. Pure, dry, biodynamic; the maison's everyday Bas-Rhin Riesling.

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Domaine OstertagAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Andre Ostertag's Riesling from volcanic-sandstone Muenchberg Grand Cru above Nothalten. Sharp, smoky, structured; a Lower Vosges Riesling worth seeking out.

★★★★★★★★★★

Paul Blanck's Furstentum Riesling from old limestone vines (old vines, vieilles vignes). Concentrated and saline; one of the maison's flagship Grand Crus.

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Paul Blanck et FilsAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Paul Blanck's flagship Schlossberg Riesling from old vines on Kientzheim granite. The maison was the first to bottle Schlossberg as a single-vineyard wine from 1976.

★★★★★★★★★★

Maison Pierre Sparr SuccesseursAlsace€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Pierre Sparr's house Reserve Cremant Brut, a multi-grape blend with a touch of Chardonnay (permitted in Cremant d Alsace). Friendly, accessible Alsace sparkling.

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Domaine Mure - Clos Saint-LandelinAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Mure's Clos Saint Landelin is a monopole inside the Vorbourg Grand Cru, south of Colmar. The Riesling is concentrated, saline, structured by the cuvee's limestone-marl.

Rene Mure Cremant d Alsace Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Festive · Easygoing

Domaine Mure - Clos Saint-LandelinAlsace€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Mure's house Cremant brut, a Pinot-family blend with a splash of Riesling for lift. Reliable traditional-method Alsace bubbles at a friendly price.

Domaines SchlumbergerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Schlumberger's flagship Kitterle Riesling from terraced sandstone vineyards above Guebwiller, often planted on slopes that need monorail access. Smoky, dry, structured.

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Domaine SchoffitAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

Schoffit's flagship Riesling from the Clos Saint Theobald inside Grand Cru Rangen, the southernmost Alsace volcano. Smoky and intensely mineral, structured for decades.

★★★★★★★★★★

Sylvie Spielmann's Altenberg de Bergheim Riesling from calcareous Bergheim slopes. Biodynamic, dry, structured; one of the rare female-run domaines in Alsace.

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant

Domaine Trapet AlsaceAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Jean-Louis Trapet's Schoenenbourg Riesling, an Alsace branch of the famous Gevrey-Chambertin family. Biodynamic, dry, structured; a Burgundian eye on Alsace.

★★★★★★★★★★

Maison TrimbachAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Trimbach's house-style Riesling, blending fruit from multiple villages around Ribeauville. Bone-dry, citrus and stone, the textbook introduction to Alsace Riesling.

Valentin Zusslin's Clos Liebenberg monopole Riesling, on south-facing limestone above Orschwihr. Biodynamic and structured, with the salinity of a hillside-walled vineyard.

Stoeffler's Kirchberg de Barr Riesling from limestone slopes around Barr. Biodynamic, dry, with the cru's clay-limestone weight.

Domaine WeinbachAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

Weinbach's flagship dry Riesling from old vines on Schlossberg granite, picked late on Saint Catherine's Day (25 November). The estate's most precise Riesling.

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Domaine WeinbachAlsace€€€€€€€€€Still White

Weinbach's top Schlossberg parcel selection from the very oldest Schlossberg vines (planted in the 1940s). Released only in vintages that yield exceptional ripeness with retained acidity.

Zind-Humbrecht's flagship Riesling from the Clos Saint Urbain inside Grand Cru Rangen, the volcanic-slope masterpiece. Smoky, intensely mineral, structured for two decades.

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Domaine Zind-HumbrechtAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Zind-Humbrecht's Clos Windsbuhl monopole sits high above Hunawihr on Muschelkalk limestone. A cool-climate, slow-developing Riesling, structured for a long cellar.

Cuvée page →

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Refreshing · Elegant

Domaine Jean-Paul ThevenetBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Beaujolais Blanc

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Domaine Romain ZordanBeaujolais€€€€€€Still White

Zordan's Beaujolais Blanc from unoaked Chardonnay: light and crisp with white-peach and citrus-blossom freshness, clean mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais Blanc

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Domaine du Vissoux (Pierre-Marie Chermette)Beaujolais€€€€€€Still White

Vissoux's Chardonnay from Pierres Dorées limestone soils: precise, mineral-driven Beaujolais Blanc with white peach and chalky freshness in the understated house style.

Beaujolais Blanc Chardonnay

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Terres Dorées' Chardonnay from the golden limestone of Pierres Dorées: between Mâcon and Chablis in character, with peach-blossom freshness, chalky mineral and restrained richness.

Chiroubles

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Refreshing · Easygoing

Domaine Damien CoqueletBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Refreshing · Elegant

Domaine Lafarge-VialBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Roussette

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Named for the golden-limestone colour of the Pierres Dorées hillsides, Roussette is Terres Dorées' richest white: beeswax, ripe peach and hazelnut character from old-barrel Chardonnay on the golden stone terroir.

Cuvée page →

Aile d'Argent

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

White wine of Mouton Rothschild from 7 hectares of sandy-gravelly soils, reviving a historic Medoc tradition. Rich and complex; Sauvignon Blanc provides drive while Semillon adds texture; partial barrel fermentation contributes subtle vanilla without overwhelming fruit.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Single-plot Semillon from 75-year-old vines in a three-hectare block; the house premium white. Dry and elegant with the waxy, textured Semillon character that recalls white Pessac-Leognan at a fraction of the price. Anne Krebiehl MW-praised.

Cuvée page →

Blanc de Lynch-Bages

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still White

Historically one of the first white wines produced by a Grand Cru Classe in the Medoc. Primarily Sauvignon Blanc driven with Semillon body; partially barrel-fermented but retains freshness through tank-vinified component.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Sauvignon Blanc

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Bauduc's benchmark white and most popular wine: 100% Sauvignon Blanc, stainless steel fermented for maximum freshness and varietal definition. Crisp, dry, and food-versatile; house white at Rick Stein's fish restaurants for over a decade.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Brion Blanc

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

One of the world's rarest and most sought-after dry whites, Haut-Brion Blanc blends Semillon richness with Sauvignon Blanc precision. White peach, orange blossom, and mineral salinity build to a honeyed, lingering finish.

Cuvée page →

Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion Blanc

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

Originally sold as Laville Haut-Brion until 2009, this is among the most complex dry whites of Bordeaux. Semillon richness and Sauvignon Blanc freshness fuse with 100% new oak to produce a wine of extraordinary mineral precision and creamy depth.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pape Clement Blanc

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still White

A classified Graves white from one of Bordeaux's most historic estates. Sauvignon Blanc leads with fresh pink grapefruit and lime blossom; Semillon adds body and Sauvignon Gris a distinctive floral note. 100% new oak fermentation produces a rich, mineral, long-lived dry white.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte Blanc

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still White

A consistently top-rated Pessac-Leognan white dominated by Sauvignon Blanc with distinctive Sauvignon Gris character. Honeyed grapefruit, crushed stone, and elderflower on the nose; vibrant citrus and mineral freshness on the palate. Frequently earns 95-98 points in recent vintages.

Cuvée page →

Cos d'Estournel Blanc

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

One of the most prestigious white Bordeaux wines from Saint-Estephe, produced from a 10-hectare dedicated white wine parcel. Fresh, vibrant, and barrel-fermented; the Sauvignon Blanc drive and Semillon texture achieve remarkable complexity.

Cuvée page →

Le Blanc de Chateau de la Riviere

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

An unusual Bordeaux Blanc built on Sauvignon Gris rather than Semillon, giving tropical and citrus character with a rich, textured palate. Only 5,600 bottles produced annually; fermented mostly in stainless steel to preserve freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Lys de Lafaurie-Peyraguey

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Lafaurie-PeyragueyBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

A dry white Bordeaux introduced in 2014 from the Lafaurie-Peyraguey estate in Bommes. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant with Semillon adding body; white flowers, lemon blossom, and fresh citrus lead a mineral, herb-tinged palate. An accessible entry into the Sauternes estate's terroir as a dry expression.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

Barrel-fermented Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from Cheval Blanc's estate, classified as Bordeaux Blanc since Saint-Emilion AOC prohibits white varieties. Elegant citrus and flint profile with saline length.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Blanc

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

The second white wine of Smith Haut Lafitte. Sauvignon Blanc-driven with Semillon adding roundness; lime tree, verbena, and citrus oil aromas lead a fresh, floral palate with elderflower and white peach. Vibrant acidity makes it an excellent aperitif and shellfish partner.

Cuvée page →

Pavillon Blanc du Château Margaux

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare Sauvignon Blanc white Bordeaux of extraordinary mineral precision: gunsmoke, slate and saline drive a taut citrus frame that ages beautifully over a decade or more.

Cuvée page →

R de Rieussec

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau RieussecBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

The dry white wine of Chateau Rieussec, a relative newcomer first produced in 1999. A fresh, citrussy Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from the Fargues-de-Langon terroir with herbal and mineral freshness; lighter-styled than the Sauternes grand vin but sharing its Semillon richness.

Cuvée page →

Y de Yquem

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Chateau d'YquemBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

The singular dry white wine of Chateau d'Yquem, made only in years when the estate decides against a botrytised grand vin or in addition to it. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant, barrel-fermented in 50% new oak, offering honeyed citrus, white peach, and mineral depth unique to the Sauternes terroir.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing

Domaine Jean-Marc BrocardBurgundy€€€€€€Still White

A rare Burgundy Sauvignon Blanc, zesty and herbal with citrus, gooseberry and a fresh finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Domaine de la VougeraieBurgundy€€€€€€€€€Still White

A historic walled white monopole, rich and saline with citrus, white flowers and a stony depth.

Billecart-Salmon Cuvee Nicolas Francois

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Champagne Billecart-SalmonChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

The house prestige cuvee: fresh bread and citrus oil over yellow orchard fruit, verbena and macadamia, full-bodied and chiseled with a vinous core cloaking brisk acids.

Bollinger La Grande Annee

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Contemplative

Champagne BollingerChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Bollinger's barrel-fermented vintage cuvee: a marine nose of sea breeze, oyster shell and gunpowder smoke over hazelnut, with orchard fruit and fine vanilla on a savoury palate.

Charles Heidsieck Blanc des Millenaires

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Charles HeidsieckChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A toasty, tropical vintage Blanc de Blancs: candied lemon and baked pineapple with coffee and floral tones, the round palate cut by minerality and high acidity on a long, pure, dry finish.

Charles Heidsieck Brut Reserve

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Charles HeidsieckChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A richly mature non-vintage built on a large reserve component: ripe lemon and pineapple over buttery pastry and vanilla toast, with textural richness lifted by a saline, chalky finish.

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Champagne DeutzChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Deutz's Pinot-led prestige cuvee: ripe orchard fruit over toast and spice, the palate vinous and deep with mineral structure and a long finish.

★★★★★★★★★★

Moet & ChandonChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A single-vintage prestige cuvee of silky, sculpted precision: white peach and candied citrus over a smoky, saline mineral core that the house calls its first plenitude.

★★★★★★★★★★

Moet & ChandonChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Dom Perignon held to its second plenitude: a generous, plump wine of slate and crushed rock over apricot, honeysuckle and mint, with a stretched, energetic glide.

KrugChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

A multi-vintage blend of extraordinary breadth: expressive toast and brioche over stone fruit, an unctuous creamy palate and a crisp mouthwatering lemon finish.

Krug Vintage

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Contemplative · Savoury

KrugChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Krug's expression of a single year: citrus and stone fruit over toasted nuts, spice and honey, with vinous depth, fine chalk and a complex, savoury, very long finish.

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Champagne Laurent-PerrierChampagne€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Laurent-Perrier's flagship blends three great vintages: lemon sherbet, ripe pear and yellow plum over lime, peach and chalk, with intense fruit, stylish restraint and toasty layers.

★★★★★★★★★★

Louis RoedererChampagne€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Roederer's perpetual-reserve multi-vintage brut: orchard fruit, chamomile and crushed rocks over brisk acids, the palate showing mature dried fruit, yeastiness and a savoury umami style.

★★★★★★★★★★

Louis RoedererChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

The original prestige cuvee, created for a tsar: zesty candied lemon and pollen over roasted hazelnut and vanilla, with incredible tension and a chalky, honed precision.

Louis RoedererChampagne€€€€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Cristal in rose form: fine red fruit and pink grapefruit over chalk and spice, with a silky texture and the same saline, racy length as the white.

Unfiltered direct-from-solera Manzanilla with richer yeasty texture and more pronounced flor character than the standard Solear; released seasonally.

Soleras Familiares Amontillado

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Bodegas Gutiérrez ColosíaJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Premium Amontillado from Gutiérrez Colosía's oldest family solera butts: the Atlantic El Puerto microclimate gives the wine a lighter, more delicate character than Jerez city-centre equivalents at comparable age.

★★★★★★★★★★

González ByassJerez & Sherry€€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Seasonally released unfiltered version of Tío Pepe drawn from the most active flor scales. Greater texture and flor complexity than the standard bottling.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing

González ByassJerez & Sherry€€€€€€Fortified Sherry

Weingut Maximin GrunhausMosel€€€€€€€Still White

★★★★★★★★★★

M. ChapoutierRhone Valley€€€€€€€€Still White

The historic assemblage white Hermitage from Chapoutier. 100% Marsanne unified across multiple parcels, producing a richly textured white that is more accessible than De l'Oree.

★★★★★★★★★★

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Firriato's entry white blends the native almond-citrus character of Catarratto with Chardonnay's stone-fruit roundness in a clean, everyday Sicilian white from the Trapani estate.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Gaudensius Etna Brut

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Festive · Elegant

Firriato Cavanera EtneaSicily€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Gaudensius is Firriato's traditional-method sparkling wine from Nerello Mascalese on the Verzella contrada: fine persistent bubbles, red-cherry vibrancy and volcanic mineral character - a strong contender alongside Murgo's long-established Etna sparkling.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen MunJebel Bianco

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€Still White

MunJebel Bianco from Cornelissen shows the volcanic-herbal dimension of Etna whites when made without additions: textured, complex and with a distinctively wild herbal-mineral character from the blend of Carricante, Catarratto and ancient Minella.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen Susucaru

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Funky

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€Still Rose

Susucaru is Cornelissen's zero-addition rosato: brief skin contact on Nerello Mascalese yields a pale, mineral-driven rose with wild red fruit and Etna's characteristic volcanic salinity.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A refreshing, organically farmed Etna Bianco from Russo's north-slope holdings: Carricante-led with bright citrus, saline mineral and the clean volcanic freshness of the estate.

Cuvée page →

Graci Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GraciSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A bright, citrus-driven Etna Bianco blending Carricante and Catarratto: saline, mineral, refreshing - capturing the north slope's volcanic energy in a fresh and approachable white.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Salina Rosso

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€Still Red

Hauner's Salina Rosso is a rare Aeolian red from Nero d'Avola and the indigenous Corinto Nero: light-bodied, herb-scented and saline-mineral, reflecting the island's volcanic coastal terroir in a red wine format.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Grillo

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's coastal Grillo from Menfi shows the grape's Mediterranean character: sun-warmed citrus blossom and peach with sea-salt mineral and high acidity from close proximity to the Tyrrhenian coast.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Terre di Giumara Fiano

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's single-vineyard Fiano from the Terre di Giumara estate shows the variety's characteristic nutty-mineral quality from Menfi's calcareous-clay soils close to the Mediterranean coast.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Grappoli del Grillo

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Grappoli del Grillo is De Bartoli's dry still table wine from Grillo, made without oxidation: a fresh, mineral white that showcases the grape's natural salinity, citrus character and almond-bitter finish when vinified as a conventional still wine rather than a perpetuo.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Pietra Pumice

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Marco De Bartoli - Bukkuram (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Pietra Pumice (pumice stone) is De Bartoli's dry Zibibbo table wine from Pantelleria: made without additions on the volcanic pumice soils, it shows the wild mineral character of the island when Zibibbo is vinified dry and naturally.

Cuvée page →

Murgo Brut Metodo Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Festive · Elegant

MurgoSicily€€€€€€€Sparkling Traditional

Sicily's most celebrated traditional-method sparkling wine: pure Nerello Mascalese fermented in bottle and aged 36-plus months on lees, producing fine bubbles, red-berry freshness and volcanic mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Murgo Etna Bianco

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

MurgoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Murgo's Etna Bianco is a bright, fresh expression of eastern-slope Carricante and Catarratto: floral, citrus-forward and mineral-clean.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Il Frappato

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Occhipinti's varietal Frappato is perhaps the finest expression of the variety: achingly delicate, chalk-mineral, fresh raspberry and rose-hip with extraordinary tension for its light weight.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti SP68 Bianco

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still White

The white counterpart to SP68 Rosso: Grecanico and Zibibbo from volcanic soils, aromatically intense, mineral and saline with a citrus-peach freshness.

Cuvée page →

Palmento Costanzo Mofete Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Palmento CostanzoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Mofete Bianco is the Palmento Costanzo estate white: bright and mineral-driven from organically farmed Carricante on Etna's north slope in Contrada Santo Spirito.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Passobianco

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still White

Chardonnay at 1,000 metres on Etna's volcanic soils: a rare and singular white - tropical richness undercut by volcanic salinity and a cool high-altitude acidity that gives exceptional freshness.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Archineri Etna Bianco

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€Still White

Archineri is Pietradolce's benchmark Etna Bianco Superiore: pure Carricante of nervy mineral tension, citrus brightness and the chalky volcanic finesse that defines Milo-area white wines.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Etna Bianco

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A fresh, immediately drinkable Etna Bianco: citrus-forward with Carricante's characteristic volcanic salinity and clean mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Chardonnay

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Bold

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's barrique-aged Chardonnay from Ulmo at Menfi became one of the wines that announced Sicily's quality transformation in the 1990s: rich, buttery, confident, with Sicilian sunshine layered through Burgundian oak treatment.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Cometa

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Cometa is Sicily's signature Fiano: Planeta introduced Campanian Fiano to Menfi soils and produced a wine distinct from Southern Italian Fiano with a mineral-salt and almond-toasted character specific to the Mediterranean coast.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Grillo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's varietal Grillo from the Menfi estate shows the Mediterranean coastal character of the grape: citrus verbena, sea-salt mineral and herbal freshness with the clean precision of Planeta's winemaking.

Cuvée page →

Planeta La Segreta Bianco

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

La Segreta Bianco blends native Grecanico with aromatic international varieties in Planeta's most accessible white: fresh, aromatic and immediately appealing with stone-fruit and citrus freshness.

Planeta Sciaranuova Carricante

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Planeta Sciaranuova (Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's Etna Carricante from the Sciaranuova estate: the volcanic freshness of the north slope expressed through Planeta's clean, precise winemaking style.

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta RapitalaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Rapitala Grand Cru is the estate's flagship Chardonnay: a full barrique-fermented Sicilian Chardonnay from the Camporeale hillside estate combining tropical fruit richness with a toasty-creamy Burgundian framework.

Cuvée page →

Serragghia Zibibbo Secco

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Serragghia (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Serragghia's dry Zibibbo is among the most mineral and wild table wines produced in Sicily: zero-sulphur, biodynamic, from the volcanic pumice soils of Pantelleria, it captures the island's raw elemental character without sweetness.

Tasca d'Almerita Chardonnay

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Tasca's Chardonnay from the high Regaleali plateau shows what Sicilian sun and altitude do to Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than Burgundy but retaining the Regaleali site's mineral backbone.

Tasca d'Almerita Leone d'Almerita

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Leone d'Almerita is the everyday entry point of the Tasca range: Catarratto and Grillo in a fresh, clean aromatic white for versatile food matching.

Tasca d'Almerita Nozze d'Oro

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Created for the fiftieth anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca's wedding, Nozze d'Oro pairs Inzolia's stone-fruit softness with Sauvignon Blanc's aromatic herbal freshness in an enduring premium Sicilian white.

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Sicily's most widely distributed native-white blend: Regaleali Bianco is a reliable, aromatic white of citrus and stone fruit freshness from the island's historic Tasca estate.

From the Milo contrada entitled to the Etna Bianco Superiore designation, 'A Puddara (Sicilian for 'la pullera', hen with chicks) is Fessina's single-vineyard Carricante of pure mineral tension and volcanic chalk finesse.

Erse is Fessina's entry-level Etna Bianco: bright, mineral-driven Carricante from the organically farmed Rovittello estate at 670 metres, with the clean saline freshness of north-slope white Etna.

Cuvée page →

Adami Vigneto Giardino Rive di Colbertaldo Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

One of the most admired single-vineyard Rive expressions in the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG. The Giardino parcel sits on the steep Colbertaldo hillside; steep gradient, poor soils and attentive pruning give the wine its notable concentration and precision.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Crede Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship multi-varietal Valdobbiadene Brut from Bisol. 'Crede' refers to the clay-limestone (crete) soils of the Valdobbiadene hills. Inclusion of indigenous Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta adds aromatic complexity absent in single-varietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Filanda Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Filanda (silk mill) is the top non-Cartizze label from Bortolomiol, employing a longer extended Charmat contact for added yeast-derived complexity. A brut style with more depth than the entry-level range.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Custoza La Selva Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Cantina di CustozaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

La Selva is the cooperative's premium single-vineyard selection from older vines in the Selva parcel. Partial oak fermentation adds texture and complexity beyond the standard cooperative Custoza, demonstrating the appellation's premium potential.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The cooperative's entry-level Soave Classico DOC provides excellent access to the appellation's almond-blossom Garganega character at very accessible price; clean, fresh and food-versatile.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Rocca Sveva is the flagship quality expression from the Cantina di Soave cooperative; a consistent, well-made Garganega-dominant Superiore DOCG that provides excellent value access to the appellation's almond-mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry Brut Valdobbiadene DOCG from Col Vetoraz. The estate's altitude above Santo Stefano produces a distinctly cool-climate Glera with sharper acidity and finer aromatics than lower-valley peers.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Festive · Elegant

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Col Vetoraz is uniquely positioned above Santo Stefano at the edge of the Cartizze hill, giving its Cartizze expression exceptional altitude and south-facing exposure. The result is an opulent, aromatic Cartizze with great aromatic intensity and length.

Cuvée page →

Fasoli Gino Le Bine Soave Superiore DOCG

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Fasoli GinoVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Le Bine is the top single-vineyard Soave Superiore DOCG from Fasoli Gino's organically farmed estate. 'Bine' are the traditional wooden stakes used for old Garganega training; this label celebrates the estate's old-vine heritage from individual pergola-trained parcels on volcanic basalt.

Cuvée page →

Fasoli Gino Soave Classico Mael

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Fasoli GinoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Mael is the flagship Soave Classico from Fasoli Gino, one of the Soave zone's pioneering certified organic estates. ICEA-certified organic viticulture at Colognola ai Colli on volcanic basalt soils gives this Garganega a particularly expressive mineral and orchard-fruit profile.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Gran Cuvee Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Gran Cuvee uses extended Charmat contact to develop yeast-derived bread and cream complexity in the Prosecco Superiore framework. A premium brut with more depth than the house standard Brut.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The house Brut from Foss Marai at Guia di Valdobbiadene. The Guia area has some of the steepest terraced vineyards in the DOCG zone; the Brut reflects this with a crisp, mineral precision.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Gini's estate Soave Classico 100% Garganega captures the family's 17th-century vine heritage in a fresh, mineral, everyday expression; the racy acidity and almond-blossom purity of the volcanic Monteforte basalt terroir at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico La Frosca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

La Frosca from the Gini family's oldest Garganega vines on the highest basalt-volcanic slopes of Monteforte d'Alpone; the chalky mineral texture, fennel herb and white-peach fruit represent the Classico zone's basalt terroir in its purest expression.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Tacchetto Bardolino Chiaretto Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Romantic

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€Still Rose

Tacchetto is a single-vineyard Bardolino Chiaretto from the estate's oldest Corvina vineyard. Chiaretto (meaning 'light' or 'pale') is the rosato of Garda, made by short skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara. Pale salmon-pink with delicate berry and rose aromatics.

Cuvée page →

Inama Bradisismo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Bradisismo (Italian for seismic land subsidence) is Inama's prestige selection from the oldest basalt-grown Garganega vines, aged 18-24 months in French barriques; arguably the most complex Soave available, with extraordinary tension between rich oak ageing and the grape's saline mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Inama Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo is the estate's second single-vineyard Soave Superiore from the adjacent San Lorenzo parcel, showing the same volcanic basalt minerality as Foscarino but with a slightly more herbal, fennel-accented character.

Cuvée page →

Inama Soave Classico Superiore Vigneti di Foscarino

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Vigneti di Foscarino is Inama's pure basalt-volcanic Soave Classico from the historic Foscarino hill, the most volcanically active terroir in the Classico zone; 100% Garganega barrel-fermented for a mineral, almond-hazelnut complexity of striking length.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Il Tralcetto Pinot Grigio delle Venezie

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The volume accessible white from Italo Cescon. Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is the broad appellation for Pinot Grigio from the Tre Venezie; the Cescon version is produced from Piave valley floor vineyards and represents the commercial, food-versatile tier of the estate alongside the character-driven Raboso.

Cuvée page →

Monte Tondo Soave Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Monte TondoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Monte Tondo's entry-level Soave Classico DOC from vineyards within the historic Classico zone. A clean, accessible Garganega-Trebbiano blend for everyday drinking; lighter than the Casette Foscarin Superiore.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Brut Nature Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Nino Franco's zero-dosage Brut Nature expression for consumers wanting the driest, most mineral face of Valdobbiadene DOCG Glera. Under 3 g/l residual sugar and racy acidity make this one of the most food-focused Proseccos in the portfolio.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Grave di Stecca Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

A single-hill Cartizze grand cru expression from the Stecca parcel, with richer fruit, honeyed florality and greater complexity than the standard Rustico, yet retaining the hallmark lightness of the hill.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Primo Franco Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The vintage-dated prestige cuvee of Nino Franco named for founder Primo Franco. Brut in style, with greater texture and a longer Charmat contact than Rustico; mineral and elegant.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Rive di Pergola Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Rive designation identifies a single-municipality harvest on steep hillsides. Di Pergola offers stony minerality and tighter acidity than the multi-commune Rustico, reflecting the specific clay-limestone terroir.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Rustico Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The benchmark entry-level Prosecco Superiore DOCG from Nino Franco. Pale straw with fine persistent perlage; white stone fruit and floral notes lead to a crisp, dry finish.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Molceo Lugana Riserva DOC

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Molceo is among the most complex white wines produced in the Lugana DOC. Extended lees contact in both oak and steel develops remarkable textural richness and autolytic complexity in the Turbiana grape. One of the first producers to prove that Lugana could produce serious age-worthy whites.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pieropan's estate Soave Classico is the reference-standard expression of the appellation: Garganega's characteristic almond-blossom and white-peach aromas with a saline, mineral finish from the volcanic basalt hillside soils of the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico Calvarino

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Calvarino is Pieropan's basalt and limestone single-vineyard Soave, planted in 1924 to Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave; the volcanic basalt soils give a crackling acidity and mineral depth far beyond the appellation average, with exceptional ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico La Rocca

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

La Rocca is 100% Garganega from the volcanic tufa hillside above Soave castle, barrel-fermented and aged; arguably Italy's most cellar-worthy white wine, developing hazelnut-honey complexity over 10-plus years while retaining the grape's almond-mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Pra Monte Grande Soave Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PraVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Pra's Monte Grande from the basalt-rich Monteforte hillside is a benchmark organic Soave Classico: Graziano Pra's organic viticulture and extended lees contact produce a pure, mineral, textural wine of genuine complexity and age-worthiness for the price.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi Capitel Croce

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Capitel Croce is Anselmi's barrel-aged 100% Garganega, named for a wayside cross on the Capitel Croce hill; oak-fermented with extended lees contact to produce a richer, more complex expression of the varietal with hazelnuts, golden apple and creamy texture.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi San Vincenzo

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€Still White

San Vincenzo is Anselmi's most widely distributed wine, a Garganega-Chardonnay Veneto IGT that captures the almond-blossom freshness of the Soave zone in a light, food-friendly, affordable white. The Chardonnay adds body without altering the Garganega character.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's estate Soave Classico is a fresh, mineral, everyday expression of Garganega from the village of Soave; classic almond-blossom aromatics with a crisp, saline finish perfect for aperitivo or seafood.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico Monte Carbonare

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's Monte Carbonare from the village of Soave itself: 100% Garganega from a steep volcanic tufa hillside with century-old vines; lees-aged in large casks producing exceptional mineral depth, citrus-almond precision and great cellaring potential.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Pinot Bianco Colli Euganei

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pinot Bianco on the volcanic soils of the Euganean Hills produces a wine of notable mineral freshness. The volcanic basalt structure of the Colli Euganei translates into a flinty mineral quality in white wines rarely seen in the Veneto plains.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Opere Trevigiane Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Opere Trevigiane is Villa Sandi's premium DOCG Brut, positioned between Il Fresco DOC and the Cartizze grand cru. It uses Glera from selected hillside parcels within the Valdobbiadene DOCG zone for a more structured brut expression.

Cuvée page →

The estate's most structured Grüner Veltliner Smaragd from the Viessling site; full body, racy acidity and deep granite-mineral character defined by biodynamic farming and old-barrel ageing.

Weingut Holzapfel PrandtauerhofWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

Classic Joching-area Grüner Veltliner Smaragd from Holzapfel: white pepper and ripe pear with full body and clean, mineral finish.

Holzapfel Joching Riesling Smaragd Achleiten

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Weingut Holzapfel PrandtauerhofWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

Holzapfel's Achleiten Riesling Smaragd shows the characteristic amphibolite-spice of this famous Ried: apricot, exotic stone-mineral and lime oil with firm acid and length.

Weingut Holzapfel PrandtauerhofWachau€€€€€€€€Still White

The Liebenberg Ried Riesling Smaragd from the historic Prandtauerhof estate in Joching: apricot, lime zest and fresh herbs with a mineral, medium-bodied palate.
